content:
You may have a corrupt index for that folder. To repair it, right-click on its name in the Folders Pane, choose Properties, General Information tab, then click the "Repair Folder" button. You may see some previously deleted messages reappear, but the index should now be correct. Compact the folder for good measure.

If that doesn't do it, let us know - there's a slightly more technical fix, but hopefully that won't be necessary.
creator: rednoise

content: Please understand, it is not thunderbird, but the network of your email provider. Thunderbird attempts to save, but it is refused. This isn't a common issue, so I would focus on your email provider's network — and I won't be surprised if they swear their innocence. Good luck.
creator: davidsk

content: David - thanks for answering!!! Yes, they do indeed swear they are innocent. They said it was probably Thunderbird, and if not call Verizon (our ISP)... And they use as evidence the fact that when I access thunderbird on the server via a browser when I'm away, then all sent items are automatically saved. Are you sure there is nothing else to try for Thunderbird?
creator: rachelb2

content: You cannot access Thunderbird on the server via a browser, although you can certainly access mail. What I mean is that the network path with webmail (for all email providers, not just this one) is different from the process when using an email client. Email clients (not just thunderbird) are forced to use the servers and settings provided by the email provider. Yes, you are impacted by your ISP and also by your email provider. Thunderbird is only an agent, attempting to send mail to the network. Good luck. :) You can try going to the advanced server button on 'server settings' pane for the account and reduce the number of settings, but that is, at best, a compromise with a poor network connection. Good luck. :)
creator: davidsk

content: This is not a Thunderbird issue. What your OS is attempting is to find the program that is set to open files with the .net suffix. For example, wordprocessing documents end in .doc or .dox x, and spreadsheets tend to use the .xls suffix. So, I suggest you contact the sender to find out what program to use for a file ending in .net. That is not a thunderbird issue. Good luck. :)

content:
It sounds to me like your Grandson added his Gmail account to Thunderbird so he could read his email (he could have just used web mail with your web browser, but be that as it may...) Is he still there? Is it necessary that his email account remain listed on your computer's Thunderbird setup? If not, go to Accounts Settings, choose his Gmail account from the list. Make sure this is HIS GMail account, not yours

Now click the red Delete button. This will not delete his Gmail account itself or any messages that are on the Gmail server, but it will delete any copies of messages that have been downloaded to your computer.

content: My first suggestion, at least until you resolve the issue, is to set thunderbird to not update automatically. Second, I would check to see if you have older profiles. You can check that by these steps: - click help>troubleshootinginformation - scroll down to 'profiles' and click 'about:profiles' - this next screen will list all profiles. if thunderbird created a new profile on the update, you might find your missing content there. Within the profiles, look to the imapmail folder for any possible IMAP accounts, the mail folder for any POP accounts, and the mail\Local Folders folder for any local folders. I am curious why you are not losing your addressbook as well. Anyway, my suggestions may not work, but I am troubled in considering that all folders are gone. Let us know.

content:
Hello there Alan 37.


Culprit It seems that automatic cleanup from email provider on the server is the culprit. It removes messages, and it is unrelated to Thunderbird filters.

Similar Please check if other devices synced to your PC are configured to purge the Recycle Bin or 'Deleted Items' folder. These devices send commands to the server to permanently delete data at set intervals. For example, if you delete a file on your phone, the server syncs this action across your environment, subsequently removing the data from your desktop. This cycle continues across all linked devices.

content:
Hello there Leegold.




Ensure your outgoing server is configured using one of the two following secure methods: See picture .


Common Connectivity Barriers

If settings are correct but mail still fails to send, the issue is likely an interception of the outgoing connection by local security layers.

VPN Interference: Many VPN providers block outgoing SMTP traffic to prevent their IP addresses from being blacklisted for spam.

Antivirus/Firewall Inspection: Software such as Avast, Bitdefender, or Norton often utilizes "Mail Shields." These act as a transparent proxy that can scramble the handshake between your client and the server, or block Port 25 entirely.

Update Corruption: System or software updates can occasionally reset firewall rules or corrupt saved authentication tokens.


Troubleshooting Steps

Isolate the Network: Temporarily disable your VPN and attempt to send a test message.

Suspend Security Shields: Briefly disable your Antivirus "Mail Shield" or Firewall. If the email sends successfully while disabled, you must add an exception/exclusion for your email client within the antivirus settings.

Verify Authentication: Re-enter your password manually. If your provider requires Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA), ensure you are using an App-Specific Password rather than your standard login password.

Note: Port 25 is frequently blocked by Internet Service Providers (ISPs) to mitigate spam. If your current configuration uses Port 25, please migrate to Port 465 or 587 immediately.




Note Please provide the previously requested information.

Use https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/how-to-make-screenshots
creator: Googlethunderbird.
